exemplar.title: To What Extent Does Parental Divorce Affect a Child's Life Well-being?

detailedExemplarData.title: To What Extent Does Parental Divorce Affect a Child's Life Well-being?

subject.title: Psychology
subject.description:
IB Psychology is a Group 3 subject in the International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP). The course examines human behavior and mental processes through scientific inquiry, exploring biological, cognitive, and sociocultural approaches to understanding behavior. Students develop critical thinking skills while investigating contemporary psychological research, research methods, and ethical considerations in psychological practice.

The course is available at Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours) and Higher Level (HL, 240 hours), with first assessment in 2027.

Assessment consists of Paper 1 (integration of concepts, content, and contexts through short-answer and extended-response questions: SL 2 hours, HL 2 hours, SL 50%, HL 40%), Paper 2 (application of psychological knowledge to research contexts: SL 1 hour, HL 2 hours, SL 25%, HL 20%), Paper 3 (HL only: research methods and data analysis, 1 hour, 20%), and Internal Assessment (research proposal development: SL 25%, HL 20%).

strengths: The topic is communicated accurately and maintained with clear purpose throughout the report.
The research question is clearly stated and consistently referenced, reinforcing focus.
Key methodological steps are described and linked to the question, showing purposeful design.
weaknesses: The title âlife well-beingâ is ambiguous and could be more precise.
The research question remains somewhat broad and could be narrowed to specific dimensions or age ranges.
Methodology lacks explicit detail on search strategy, inclusion/exclusion criteria, and justification for sample choices.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand assessments place topic communication solidly in the 5-6 band, while question focus and methodology sit at the top of 3-4. Overall performance shows very good focus with minor imprecision. A score of 5/6 reflects strong purpose and coherence, with some room for tightening scope and methodological transparency.

strengths: Terminology (e.g., âattachment insecurity,â âmediator,â âlongitudinal designâ) is used accurately and consistently.
Theoretical foundations from attachment theory and mediation models are well integrated.
Sources are applied coherently to support arguments and demonstrate clear subject knowledge.
weaknesses: The operational definition of childrenâs well-being is not specified or linked to measurement scales.
A few citations lack full bibliographic details, reducing reliability.
Stylistic inconsistencies and occasional awkward phrasing slightly undermine precision.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Use of discipline-specific terminology and application of source material consistently meet upper-band 3-4 descriptors. Minor lapses in operational definitions and citation precision prevent a perfect score. A 5/6 appropriately rewards strong knowledge with slight room for increased rigor.

strengths: Research draws on a broad, up-to-date range of peer-reviewed studies directly linked to the question.
Conclusions are generally well supported, with clear evaluation of strengths and limitations in most sections.
The synthesis in the conclusion aligns findings to the mediation model, providing logical coherence.
weaknesses: Over-reliance on U.S. samples limits generalizability; non-Western perspectives are under-represented.
Some evaluations remain descriptive (e.g., noting sample sizes) rather than critically weighting evidence.
Discussions of effect sizes and methodological diversity could be deepened, and cultural/operational variations more critically examined.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand scores (7 for relevance, 6 for analysis, 6 for evaluation) indicate work sits at the top of the 4-6 band approaching 7-9. The overall argument is coherent and mostly critical, but occasional superficiality and limited scope prevent full 12/12. A score of 10/12 reflects strong critical thinking with targeted areas for deeper analytical rigor.

strengths: All required structural elements (title page, headings, numbered pages) are present and logically ordered.
Table of contents and visual aids (e.g., mediation model) enhance readability and navigation.
Citations and reference list follow recognized conventions and are comprehensive.
weaknesses: Minor formatting glitches (misaligned page numbers in the table of contents, duplicate reference entries) affect professional polish.
Line breaks in URLs and occasional spacing inconsistencies slightly distract from a flawless layout.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Structure and formatting meet all major conventions, placing the work in the top presentation band. Minor layout inconsistencies are non-critical. A score of 4/4 reflects fully appropriate presentation with small areas for cosmetic improvement.

strandSelections.reasoning: Psychology terms such as âattachment insecurity,â âmediator,â âinternal validity,â and âlongitudinal designâ are used correctly and regularly. A few stylistic errors and awkward phrases (e.g., âlife well-being,â âoffspringâ) show that precision is not fully consistent, but overall usage is mostly accurate, meriting the top of the 3-4 band.

Pages are numbered, a word count and title are provided, and in-text citations follow a recognisable style. Minor inconsistencies (duplicate reference entries, line breaks in URLs, occasional formatting glitches) keep the work from flawless presentation, but layout considerations are largely correct, justifying a low 3-4 band score.

The essay demonstrates solid knowledge of attachment theory, emotional security hypothesis, mediation models and cites many landmark studies. Summaries are generally accurate and sources are applied to illustrate arguments, though occasional mis-citations and surface treatment of some constructs prevent a consistently exemplary standard. Hence, upper 3-4 band is appropriate.

The student uses a literature-review approach, selecting a good number of longitudinal, meta-analytic, and twin studies and evaluating them. Yet there is limited explanation of search strategy, inclusion criteria, or justification for choosing largely U.S. samples; no primary data are collected. This âmostly completeâ but not fully articulated methodology corresponds to the lower end of the 3-4 band.

The research focusâparental divorce and child well-beingâis introduced immediately, the rationale is explained with statistics and theoretical background, and the essay maintains this focus throughout the headings and discussion. Minor wordiness and an imprecise title (âlife well-beingâ) slightly reduce clarity, but overall communication is accurate and purposefully maintained, fitting solidly in the 5-6 band.

The selection of research is broad, up-to-date, and largely peer-reviewed. Each study clearly links to the research question, with contrasting evidence from high- and low-conflict families. Minor over-reliance on U.S. data and omission of some cultural work prevent the highest level, but relevance is âmostly consistent,â placing the strand at the bottom of the 7-9 band.

The question is clearly stated and repeatedly referenced; however, âTo what extent does parental divorce affect a childâs life well-being?â remains broad and could have been narrowed to specific dimensions, age ranges, or mediators. Focus is therefore only partial, positioning the work at the top of the 3-4 band rather than fully focused (5-6).

The essay follows expected conventions: title page, abstract-style introduction, organised headings matching the table of contents, and a concluding section. Citations and reference list are present. All structural elements are included and logically ordered, fulfilling criteria for the 3-4 band.

The conclusion juxtaposes conflicting evidence (negative vs. protective effects) and ties back to the mediation model, showing reasoned argumentation. Yet the synthesis remains somewhat surface-level, with limited critical reflection on methodological diversity or wider implications. The argument is âreasoned with partially critical evaluation,â fitting the upper end of the 4-6 band.

The student evaluates strengths and limitations for nearly every cited study and integrates findings into an overall argument; however, some evaluations are descriptive (e.g., repeating sample sizes) rather than analytically weighted, and causal claims are occasionally accepted uncritically. Analysis is clear and mostly supportedâstrong for the 4-6 band but not yet âeffectiveâ enough for 7-9, so a 6 is awarded.

annotations.comment: The table of contents demonstrates clear organization through detailed headings, aiding reader navigation.
The linkage between inconsistent caregiving and attachment insecurity is concise and coherent, strengthening the theoretical foundation.

The title âExploring the Effects of Parental Divorce on a Childâs Lifeâ is broad and uses the term âlife well-being,â which is ambiguous. Consider refining to specify which dimension(s) of well-being are examined.
The research question is stated clearly but remains overly broad. Narrow by specifying age ranges, mediators, or contexts to improve focus and connection to the analysis.
Page numbers in the table of contents appear misaligned and cramped. Adjust cell spacing to improve readability and professional presentation.
The introduction defines childrenâs well-being in general terms but lacks an operational definition or measurement framework. Include specific scales or indices to clarify what is assessed.
The citation âPsychological Wellbeing in Children, 2023â omits an author or organization. Provide full source details for reliable attribution.
Theoretical grounding in attachment theory is well-articulated, clearly linking caregiving consistency to child attachment outcomes.
The discussion of Amato & Sobolewski (2001) is descriptive. Enhance critical depth by evaluating sampling decisions, potential biases, and the weight of conclusions.
The methodology description omits search strategy, databases, and inclusion/exclusion criteria. Detail these elements to demonstrate informed source selection.
Restating the research question at the start of the methodology section reinforces focus and purpose throughout the analysis.
In-text citations mix styles (e.g., âAmato and Sobolewski, 2001; Zill, Morrison, and Coiro, 1993â). Standardize to a single citation format.
Fig. 1 caption effectively describes the mediation model; the visual presentation supports the text discussion well.
A standalone URL appears outside the formatted reference list. Integrate or remove to maintain professional formatting.
The description of causal pathways is cautious, but quantitative effect sizes from the original model would deepen analytical insight.
The Zill et al. (1993) summary provides rich descriptive statistics but lacks discussion of statistical significance and effect size interpretation.
Highlighting the longitudinal designâs strength in demonstrating change over time strengthens your argument about internal validity.
The recognition of the U.S.-only sampleâs limited external validity shows strong critical awareness of generalizability issues.
While self-report bias is noted, suggest proposing data triangulation or observational methods as mitigation strategies.
Integration of the Emotional Security Hypothesis bridges theory and empirical research effectively, showing analytical depth.
Your critique on ecological validity of lab-based studies is insightful; consider suggesting naturalistic observation as an alternative.
Linking coping mechanisms to protective outcomes is well argued with clear empirical support.
The DâOnofrio and Emery (2019) sectionâs inclusion of meta-analytic and twin methods adds robustness and depth to your literature review.
When discussing subgroup analyses, reflect on potential confounding influences and how they might affect the reported outcomes.
Critique of conflict intensity measurement is apt; suggest adding behavioral observation or validated hostility scales to improve accuracy.
The study omits post-divorce factors such as custody arrangements and support payments. Address these in limitations for a more balanced methodology critique.
Strohschein (2005) section notes measurement validity issues. Also consider discussing potential reporter bias from parent-reported measures.
Your description of growth-curve modeling demonstrates solid methodological understanding and its relevance to tracking trajectories.
Musick & Meier (2010) sectionâs multidimensional outcome measures strengthen validity by capturing diverse aspects of adult well-being.
Jekielek (1998) summary effectively reports multiple regression results, demonstrating proficiency in quantitative reporting.
Suggest extending follow-up beyond four years in limitations to capture longer-term well-being trajectories.
The conclusion revisits main findings and aligns them with the mediation model, providing a coherent synthesis.
Consider discussing how differing operational definitions of well-being across studies may affect comparability and synthesis.
Your acknowledgement of U.S. sample limitations is strong; strengthen by proposing specific non-Western studies to address this gap.
Recapping the mediation model in the conclusion reinforces coherence and maintains focused argumentation.
Recommending future research involving childrenâs own voices is valuable; specify qualitative methods (e.g., interviews, focus groups) to deepen insights.
The final summary captures nuanced findings and contextual factors succinctly, providing a well-structured closing.
References show inconsistency in DOI formatting, URL breaks, and duplicate entries. Standardize style and remove redundancies.
Duplicate citation of Bradburn (1969) suggests reference list needs review; consolidate and correct entries.
The final reference list is comprehensive and demonstrates thorough literature coverage.
Reference entries include HTML/math artifacts (e.g., <math> tags). Clean up to ensure clarity and consistency.
